attachment.content:
CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- document-skills:brand-guidelines: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.
- document-skills:algorithmic-art: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
- document-skills:web-artifacts-builder: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.
- document-skills:doc-coauthoring: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.
- document-skills:theme-factory: Toolkit for styling artifacts with a theme. These artifacts can be slides, docs, reportings, HTML landing pages, etc. There are 10 pre-set themes with colors/fonts that you can apply to any artifact that has been creating, or can generate a new theme on-the-fly.
- document-skills:claude-api: Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK. TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK. DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks.
- document-skills:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- document-skills:webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
- document-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- document-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- document-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- document-skills:internal-comms: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).
- document-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- document-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- document-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- document-skills:slack-gif-creator: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like "make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack."
- document-skills:canvas-design: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. You should use this skill when the user asks to create a poster, piece of art, design, or other static piece. Create original visual designs, never copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2	name: AHBU project state

4	type: project

6	---
7	AHBU = AI-assisted Heart-Brain Understanding portfolio project. Layered Endeavor Framework with project-specific 4-layer tree:
8	
9	- `00-vision/` — root. Why we exist + hard constraints.
10	- `10-pain-point/` — discover + validate pain. One folder per candidate (`<slug>/candidate.md`, `admission.md`, `real-pain-critic.md`). Layer-shared: `shared/portfolio.md`, `validation-log.md`, historical critic outputs.
11	- `20-plan/` — pain → technical: design + interpret (methodology + analysis merged). One folder per admitted track (`approach.md`, `risk-register.md`, `protocol-lock.md`, `methodology-critic.md`, `pilots-README.md`, post-headline `findings.md` / `limitations.md` / `lessons.md`). Layer-shared: `shared/reuse-sketches/`.
12	- `30-implement/` — code, runs, results, plus `compute.md`, `datasets.md`. One folder per running track (`code/`, `runs/`, `results.md`). Layer-shared: `shared/{data,eval,models}/` (lazy materialize).
13	
14	Each pain point exists as a folder inside whichever layers it has reached.
15	
16	**As of 2026-05-03:** v2 admission rubric (real-pain only at layer 10; feasibility enforced at layer 20). All FOUR tracks admitted + layer-20 protocol-locked v2 (re-locked 2026-05-03 after pilot findings forced unlocks): `cross-subject-eeg` (HYBRID: MDM on PhysionetMI + LaBraM on Cho2017 after PhysionetMI flagged contaminated by leakage_audit), `ecg-ppg-realworld` (PIVOTED PTB-XL → CinC 2017 after only 8 AFIB records found in fold 10), `sleep-staging` (TWO co-primary headlines: HEADLINE-A MESA AHI-stratified U-Sleep N~1850 + HEADLINE-B HMC PSG HRV-vs-EEG paired N=77), `affective-state` (Path X: EDA features expanded 6→40 after cvxEDA found unavailable; N_total=126). All 4 critic-v2 PASSED-with-fixes; all fixes applied as of 2026-05-03. Layer-30 status: pilots run for 3 of 4 tracks (cross-subject-eeg P-1..P-6 done + leakage_audit; affective-state P-1, P-2, P-3 WESAD-only, P-6 done; ecg-ppg-realworld old PTB-XL P-1 + P-3 done but OBSOLETE — NEW CinC 2017 pilots P-1..P-6 not yet run; sleep-staging runs/ EMPTY — no pilots run yet). NO headline executed any track.
17	
18	**Why:** the old `layers/`/`tracks/`/`shared/`/`resources/` split had `shared/` and `resources/` as ad-hoc non-layers. New layout makes `shared/` per-layer and pulls compute/datasets into 30-implement where they're exercised.
19	
20	**How to apply:** check `10-pain-point/shared/portfolio.md` for current admission state. Critic pass required at every help-boundary milestone. Human checkpoint after each meaningful chunk — no barreling. When designing methodology, reuse-scan against `30-implement/shared/` (still empty as of 2026-05-03 — first track to ship is the first promoter). Next chunks (in priority): (D) ecg-ppg-realworld run new CinC 2017 pilots P-1..P-6 (mandatory before headline); sleep-staging run P-1 (HMC + AHI metadata audit), P-3 (U-Sleep VRAM), P-7 (NSRR token + MESA AHI distribution sanity); (E) headline pre-flight + execution per track (held-out partition touched ONCE, requires explicit go-ahead).
21

AHBU = AI-assisted Heart-Brain Understanding portfolio project. Layered Endeavor Framework with project-specific 4-layer tree:

- `00-vision/` — root. Why we exist + hard constraints.
- `10-pain-point/` — discover + validate pain. One folder per candidate (`<slug>/candidate.md`, `admission.md`, `real-pain-critic.md`). Layer-shared: `shared/portfolio.md`, `validation-log.md`, historical critic outputs.
- `20-plan/` — pain → technical: design + interpret (methodology + analysis merged). One folder per admitted track (`approach.md`, `risk-register.md`, `protocol-lock.md`, `methodology-critic.md`, `pilots-README.md`, post-headline `findings.md` / `limitations.md` / `lessons.md`). Layer-shared: `shared/reuse-sketches/`.
- `30-implement/` — code, runs, results, plus `compute.md`, `datasets.md`. One folder per running track (`code/`, `runs/`, `results.md`). Layer-shared: `shared/{data,eval,models}/` (lazy materialize).

Each pain point exists as a folder inside whichever layers it has reached.

**As of 2026-05-03:** v2 admission rubric (real-pain only at layer 10; feasibility enforced at layer 20). All FOUR tracks admitted + layer-20 protocol-locked v2 (re-locked 2026-05-03 after pilot findings forced unlocks): `cross-subject-eeg` (HYBRID: MDM on PhysionetMI + LaBraM on Cho2017 after PhysionetMI flagged contaminated by leakage_audit), `ecg-ppg-realworld` (PIVOTED PTB-XL → CinC 2017 after only 8 AFIB records found in fold 10), `sleep-staging` (TWO co-primary headlines: HEADLINE-A MESA AHI-stratified U-Sleep N~1850 + HEADLINE-B HMC PSG HRV-vs-EEG paired N=77), `affective-state` (Path X: EDA features expanded 6→40 after cvxEDA found unavailable; N_total=126). All 4 critic-v2 PASSED-with-fixes; all fixes applied as of 2026-05-03. NO headline executed any track.

**Why:** the old `layers/`/`tracks/`/`shared/`/`resources/` split had `shared/` and `resources/` as ad-hoc non-layers. New layout makes `shared/` per-layer and pulls compute/datasets into 30-implement where they're exercised.

**How to apply:** check `10-pain-point/shared/portfolio.md` for current admission state. Critic pass required at every help-boundary milestone. # Portfolio registry

The active state of the AHBU pain-point portfolio. Each candidate has a status; transitions are dated and reasoned.

**Status values:** `candidate` (under investigation) · `admitted` (track active) · `deferred` (failed admission gate, conditions for re-eval listed) · `retired` (track completed).

## Active

| Slug | Status | Track dir | Admitted | Layer-20 status | Layer-30 status | Notes |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-----------------|-----------------|-------|
| `cross-subject-eeg` | admitted | `30-implement/cross-subject-eeg/` | 2026-05-02 | protocol-lock v2 (2026-05-03), critic-v2 pass-with-fixes applied | pilots P-1..P-6 + leakage_audit done | HYBRID: MDM on PhysionetMI + LaBraM on Cho2017 after PhysionetMI contamination found. Headline not yet run. |
| `affective-state` | admitted | `30-implement/affective-state/` | 2026-05-02 | protocol-lock v2 (2026-05-03), critic-v2 pass-with-fixes applied | pilots P-1, P-2, P-3 (WESAD only), P-6 done | Path X: EDA expanded 6→40 features; N_total=126 (exact-binom power 0.04594). DEAP/MAHNOB-HCI access pending. Headline not yet run. |
| `ecg-ppg-realworld` | admitted | `30-implement/ecg-ppg-realworld/` | 2026-05-02 | protocol-lock v2 (2026-05-03), critic-v2 pass-with-fixes applied | OLD PTB-XL P-1, P-3 done — OBSOLETE; NEW CinC 2017 P-1..P-6 NOT YET RUN | Path C: pivoted PTB-XL → CinC 2017 after fold-10 AFIB count = 8 (need >=87). Headline not yet run. |
| `sleep-staging` | admitted | `30-implement/sleep-staging/` | 2026-05-02 | protocol-lock v2 (2026-05-03), critic-v2 pass-with-fixes applied | runs/ EMPTY — no pilots run | TWO co-primary headlines: HEADLINE-A MESA N~1850 + HEADLINE-B HMC PSG N=77. P-3 (U-Sleep VRAM) gates both; P-7 gates HEADLINE-A. Headline not yet run. |

## Candidates under investigation

_None — all 4 candidates admitted as of 2026-05-02._

## Deferred

_None yet — admission decisions still pending first round._

## Retired

_None yet._

---

## How to update

- New candidate: add row to "Candidates under investigation" + drop a `10-pain-point/<slug>/candidate.md` file.
- Admission: move row to "Active", write `<slug>/admission.md` + `<slug>/real-pain-critic.md`, instantiate `20-plan/<slug>/`, append to `validation-log.md`.
- Deferral: move row to "Deferred", record reason + re-eval conditions.
- Retirement: move row to "Retired" once `20-plan/<slug>/lessons.md` is signed off; surface lessons to the appropriate layer's `shared/` or to `lessons.md`.

content.content:
1	> **Spec:** originating-track promotion event (ecg-ppg-realworld P-4) + expected second-consumer (sleep-staging or affective-state confidence stratification).
2	
3	# `shared/eval/` — selective classification utilities
4	
5	This is the **first promotion** to `30-implement/shared/`. Per the
6	CLAUDE.md "promote eagerly once >=1 plausible second consumer exists"
7	discipline.
8	
9	## Promotion event
10	
11	- **Originating track / consumer:** `ecg-ppg-realworld` pilot P-4
12	  (3-epoch xresnet1d50 + temperature scaling + abstention sweep on
13	  CinC 2017 dev validation hold-out). The `pilots-README.md#P-4`
14	  spec calls this utility out by name as
15	  `30-implement/shared/eval/abstention.py:selective_classification_curve()`,
16	  so the abstraction is established at design time, not opportunistically.
17	- **Expected second consumer:** any track that surfaces a
18	  PPV-vs-coverage or selective-classification figure. Concrete near-term
19	  candidates:
20	  - `sleep-staging` — confidence stratification on epoch-level stage
21	    predictions (a candidate finding from that track's protocol-lock).
22	  - `affective-state` — abstention on uncertain emotion classifications
23	    (mentioned in that track's risk register).
24	- **Headline consumer (this track):** `ecg-ppg-realworld` headline
25	  also calls this utility through `code/headline/` to compute the
26	  PPV-at-coverage curve for the AF-vs-rest binary task.
27	
28	## Files
29	
30	- `abstention.py` — `selective_classification_curve(confidences,
31	  correct, coverages)` returning per-coverage threshold,
32	  achieved coverage, n_committed, and accuracy on the committed
33	  subset. Also returns the AUROC of confidence vs correctness as a
34	  summary statistic (the "confidence-correctness AUC" in P-4 success
35	  criteria).
36	- `ppv_at_coverage.py` — `ppv_at_coverage(y_true, y_pred,
37	  confidences, coverages, bootstrap_n=2000, bootstrap_seed=42)`
38	  returning per-coverage PPV with stratified bootstrap 95% CI. PPV is
39	  computed on the predicted-positive subset within the committed set.
40	- `__init__.py` — re-exports both functions.
41	
42	## Usage
43	
44	```python
45	from shared.eval import selective_classification_curve, ppv_at_coverage
46	
47	# Confidence vs correctness (per-record max-softmax; binary correct flag).
48	sc = selective_classification_curve(
49	    confidences=conf_array,            # shape (N,)
50	    correct=correct_array,             # shape (N,)
51	    coverages=[1.00, 0.95, 0.90, 0.85, 0.83, 0.79],
52	)
53	print(sc["auc_correctness"])           # confidence-correctness AUROC
54	for p in sc["points"]:
55	    print(p["target_coverage"], p["accuracy_on_committed"])
56	
57	# PPV at coverage, with stratified bootstrap CI.
58	ppv = ppv_at_coverage(
59	    y_true=y_true_binary,
60	    y_pred=y_pred_binary,
61	    confidences=conf_array,
62	    coverages=[1.00, 0.95, 0.83],
63	)
64	for p in ppv["points"]:
65	    print(p["target_coverage"], p["ppv"], p["ppv_ci95_lo"], p["ppv_ci95_hi"])
66	```
67	
68	## Conventions
69	
70	- All inputs are `np.ndarray` shape `(N,)`.
71	- "Confidence" is convention-free: any score where higher = more
72	  confident works. For softmax classifiers the typical choice is
73	  `max(softmax(logits / T))` where T is a fitted temperature.
74	- "Correct" is `(y_pred == y_true)` (multi-class) or `(y_pred ==
75	  y_true)` over the binary projection — the caller decides the
76	  binarisation upstream.
77	- For PPV, the predicted-positive subset is determined by the
78	  caller's `y_pred`, not by the confidence ranking. Confidence is
79	  used only to set the coverage threshold.

file.content:
> **Spec:** originating-track promotion event (ecg-ppg-realworld P-4) + expected second-consumer (sleep-staging or affective-state confidence stratification).

# `shared/eval/` — selective classification utilities

This is the **first promotion** to `30-implement/shared/`. Per the
CLAUDE.md "promote eagerly once >=1 plausible second consumer exists"
discipline.

## Promotion event

- **Originating track / consumer:** `ecg-ppg-realworld` pilot P-4
  (3-epoch xresnet1d50 + temperature scaling + abstention sweep on
  CinC 2017 dev validation hold-out). The `pilots-README.md#P-4`
  spec calls this utility out by name as
  `30-implement/shared/eval/abstention.py:selective_classification_curve()`,
  so the abstraction is established at design time, not opportunistically.
- **Expected second consumer:** any track that surfaces a
  PPV-vs-coverage or selective-classification figure. Concrete near-term
  candidates:
  - `sleep-staging` — confidence stratification on epoch-level stage
    predictions (a candidate finding from that track's protocol-lock).
  - `affective-state` — abstention on uncertain emotion classifications
    (mentioned in that track's risk register).
- **Headline consumer (this track):** `ecg-ppg-realworld` headline
  also calls this utility through `code/headline/` to compute the
  PPV-at-coverage curve for the AF-vs-rest binary task.

## Files

- `abstention.py` — `selective_classification_curve(confidences,
  correct, coverages)` returning per-coverage threshold,
  achieved coverage, n_committed, and accuracy on the committed
  subset. Also returns the AUROC of confidence vs correctness as a
  summary statistic (the "confidence-correctness AUC" in P-4 success
  criteria).
- `ppv_at_coverage.py` — `ppv_at_coverage(y_true, y_pred,
  confidences, coverages, bootstrap_n=2000, bootstrap_seed=42)`
  returning per-coverage PPV with stratified bootstrap 95% CI. PPV is
  computed on the predicted-positive subset within the committed set.
- `__init__.py` — re-exports both functions.

## Usage

```python
from shared.eval import selective_classification_curve, ppv_at_coverage

# Confidence vs correctness (per-record max-softmax; binary correct flag).
sc = selective_classification_curve(
    confidences=conf_array,            # shape (N,)
    correct=correct_array,             # shape (N,)
    coverages=[1.00, 0.95, 0.90, 0.85, 0.83, 0.79],
)
print(sc["auc_correctness"])           # confidence-correctness AUROC
for p in sc["points"]:
    print(p["target_coverage"], p["accuracy_on_committed"])

# PPV at coverage, with stratified bootstrap CI.
ppv = ppv_at_coverage(
    y_true=y_true_binary,
    y_pred=y_pred_binary,
    confidences=conf_array,
    coverages=[1.00, 0.95, 0.83],
)
for p in ppv["points"]:
    print(p["target_coverage"], p["ppv"], p["ppv_ci95_lo"], p["ppv_ci95_hi"])
```

## Conventions

- All inputs are `np.ndarray` shape `(N,)`.
- "Confidence" is convention-free: any score where higher = more
  confident works. For softmax classifiers the typical choice is
  `max(softmax(logits / T))` where T is a fitted temperature.
- "Correct" is `(y_pred == y_true)` (multi-class) or `(y_pred ==
  y_true)` over the binary projection — the caller decides the
  binarisation upstream.
- For PPV, the predicted-positive subset is determined by the
  caller's `y_pred`, not by the confidence ranking. Confidence is
  used only to set the coverage threshold.
- Bootstrap is stratified by `y_true` to preserve prevalence in the
  CI; this matches the protocol-lock §5 convention for
  ecg-ppg-realworld.

"""
Spec: 20-plan/ecg-ppg-realworld/pilots-README.md#P-4

P-4 — Abstention threshold sweep on CinC 2017 dev validation hold-out.

Procedure:
  1. Load CinC 2017 dev split (from runs/partition.json — built by P-6).
  2. Internally split dev into 80% training / 20% validation hold-out
     (seed=42, stratified by class). Validation hold-out is the
     within-dev probe surface; the headline test split is NOT touched.
  3. Train xresnet1d50 (single-lead, 4-class) for 3 epochs at batch=8,
     fp32, Adam lr=1e-3. Inputs are 9000-sample (30 s × 300 Hz)
     pre-processed waveforms (notch + highpass + clip + z-score per
     p2_classical_baseline.preprocess()).
  4. On the validation hold-out: extract logits, compute softmax probs,
     fit temperature parameter T by Brier-score minimization
     (scipy.optimize.minimize_scalar over T in [0.5, 10]).
  5. Compute coverage-vs-PPV curve via shared.eval.selective_classification_curve()
     (binary AF-vs-rest), at coverages [1.00, 0.95, 0.90, 0.85, 0.83, 0.79].
  6. Compute confidence-correctness AUC (label = correct, score =
     post-temperature max-softmax) via sklearn.metrics.roc_auc_score.

Success criteria (per pilots-README §P-4):
  - Confidence-correctness AUC >= 0.55 (R-3 kill threshold).
  - PPV at coverage=0.83 > PPV at coverage=1.00 on the 3-epoch model.

Side-effects (saved for P-5):
  - runs/p4_xresnet1d50_3ep_<ts>.pt — model state_dict.
  - runs/p4_temperature_<ts>.json — temperature T value + metadata.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import sys
import time
import traceback
from pathlib import Path

"""
Spec: 20-plan/ecg-ppg-realworld/pilots-README.md#P-5

P-5 — Calibration baseline: Brier and ECE before vs after temperature
scaling, on the CinC 2017 within-dev validation hold-out.

Procedure:
  1. Load the trained 3-epoch model + fitted temperature T saved by P-4.
  2. Reconstruct the same within-dev validation hold-out (seed=42,
     stratified, 20% of dev).
  3. Compute Brier score and ECE (15 bins) with and without temperature
     scaling.
  4. Plot reliability diagrams side-by-side; save PNG.

Success criteria (per pilots-README §P-5):
  - ECE(calibrated) < ECE(uncalibrated).
  - Reliability diagram shows smaller deviation from diagonal post-scale.
  - T in [1.0, 5.0]. T > 5.0 is a flag (not a fail) — surfaces severe
    overconfidence in the early model that's expected to ease with full
    50-epoch training.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import glob
import json
import os
import sys
import time
import traceback
from pathlib import Path

# Ensure shared/ is importable.
_THIS = Path(__file__).resolve()
_REPO_ROOT = _THIS.parents[4]  # ahbu/
_SHARED_PARENT = _REPO_ROOT / "30-implement"
if str(_SHARED_PARENT) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(_SHARED_PARENT))

42	def preprocess(signal, fs: int):
43	    import numpy as np
44	    from scipy.signal import butter, filtfilt, iirnotch
45	
46	    sos_b, sos_a = butter(4, 0.5 / (fs / 2), btype="highpass")
47	    signal = filtfilt(sos_b, sos_a, signal)
48	    notch_b, notch_a = iirnotch(50.0 / (fs / 2), Q=30)
49	    signal = filtfilt(notch_b, notch_a, signal)
50	    signal = np.clip(signal, -5.0, 5.0)
51	    mu = float(signal.mean())
52	    sigma = float(signal.std())
53	    if sigma > 1e-9:
54	        signal = (signal - mu) / sigma
55	    return signal

80	def pad_or_crop(signal, target_len: int):
81	    import numpy as np
82	
83	    n = len(signal)
84	    if n == target_len:
85	        return signal.astype(np.float32)
86	    if n > target_len:
87	        start = (n - target_len) // 2
88	        return signal[start : start + target_len].astype(np.float32)
89	    out = np.zeros(target_len, dtype=np.float32)
90	    out[:n] = signal.astype(np.float32)

104	def latest_artifact(runs_dir: Path, pattern: str):
105	    cands = sorted(runs_dir.glob(pattern), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)

109	def compute_ece(probs, y_true, n_bins: int = 15) -> float:
110	    """Multi-class ECE: bin by max-confidence; per-bin gap = |acc - conf|."""
111	    import numpy as np
112	
113	    pred = probs.argmax(axis=1)
114	    conf = probs.max(axis=1)
115	    correct = (pred == y_true).astype(float)
116	
117	    bins = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1)
118	    ece = 0.0
119	    n = len(y_true)
120	    for i in range(n_bins):
121	        lo, hi = bins[i], bins[i + 1]
122	        # Last bin closed on right.
123	        if i == n_bins - 1:
124	            mask = (conf >= lo) & (conf <= hi)
125	        else:
126	            mask = (conf >= lo) & (conf < hi)
127	        if mask.sum() == 0:
128	            continue
129	        acc_bin = float(correct[mask].mean())
130	        conf_bin = float(conf[mask].mean())
131	        ece += (mask.sum() / n) * abs(acc_bin - conf_bin)
132	    return float(ece)
133	
134	
135	def reliability_bins(probs, y_true, n_bins: int = 15):
136	    import numpy as np
137	
138	    pred = probs.argmax(axis=1)
139	    conf = probs.max(axis=1)
140	    correct = (pred == y_true).astype(float)
141	    bins = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1)
142	    bin_acc = []
143	    bin_conf = []
144	    bin_count = []
145	    for i in range(n_bins):
146	        lo, hi = bins[i], bins[i + 1]
147	        if i == n_bins - 1:
148	            mask = (conf >= lo) & (conf <= hi)
149	        else:
150	            mask = (conf >= lo) & (conf < hi)
151	        n_in = int(mask.sum())
152	        bin_count.append(n_in)
153	        if n_in == 0:
154	            bin_acc.append(None)
155	            bin_conf.append(None)
156	        else:
157	            bin_acc.append(float(correct[mask].mean()))
158	            bin_conf.append(float(conf[mask].mean()))
159	    return bins, bin_acc, bin_conf, bin_count

162	def plot_reliability(bins, acc_uncal, conf_uncal, count_uncal, acc_cal, conf_cal, count_cal, T, out_path: Path, ece_uncal: float, ece_cal: float):
163	    import matplotlib
164	
165	    matplotlib.use("Agg")
166	    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
167	    import numpy as np
168	
169	    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 5))
170	    centers = (bins[:-1] + bins[1:]) / 2.0
171	
172	    for ax, acc, conf, count, title, ece in (
173	        (axes[0], acc_uncal, conf_uncal, count_uncal, "uncalibrated", ece_uncal),
174	        (axes[1], acc_cal, conf_cal, count_cal, f"calibrated (T={T:.2f})", ece_cal),
175	    ):
176	        ax.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", linewidth=1, alpha=0.6)
177	        # Bars: x = bin center, height = bin acc.
178	        widths = 1.0 / len(centers)
179	        heights = [a if a is not None else 0.0 for a in acc]
180	        ax.bar(centers, heights, width=widths * 0.9, alpha=0.7, edgecolor="black", label="accuracy")
181	        # Overlay confidence as small markers.
182	        for c_x, c_y in zip(centers, conf):
183	            if c_y is not None:
184	                ax.plot(c_x, c_y, "rx", markersize=8)
185	        ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
186	        ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
187	        ax.set_xlabel("confidence")
188	        ax.set_ylabel("accuracy")
189	        ax.set_title(f"{title} — ECE={ece:.4f}")
190	        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
191	
192	    fig.suptitle("Reliability diagrams — P-5 calibration baseline (CinC 2017 dev hold-out)")
193	    fig.tight_layout()
194	    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
195	    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=120)
196	    plt.close(fig)

199	def probe(
200	    cinc_dir: Path,
201	    partition_json: Path,
202	    runs_dir: Path,
203	    model_path: Path | None,
204	    temperature_path: Path | None,
205	    target_len: int,
206	    fs: int,
207	    n_bins: int,
208	):
209	    if not cinc_dir.exists():
210	        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"CinC 2017 dir not found at {cinc_dir}"}
211	    if not partition_json.exists():
212	        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"partition.json not found at {partition_json}"}
213	
214	    reference_csv = find_reference_csv(cinc_dir)
215	    if reference_csv is None:
216	        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"REFERENCE csv not found under {cinc_dir}"}
217	
218	    try:
219	        import numpy as np
220	        import pandas as pd
221	        import torch
222	        from scipy.special import softmax
223	        from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedShuffleSplit
224	    except ImportError as e:
225	        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"missing dep: {e}"}
226	
227	    if not torch.cuda.is_available():
228	        return {"status": "fail", "reason": "torch.cuda unavailable; P-5 requires GPU."}
229	
230	    # Auto-resolve P-4 artifacts.
231	    if model_path is None:
232	        model_path = latest_artifact(runs_dir, "p4_xresnet1d50_3ep_*.pt")
233	    if temperature_path is None:
234	        temperature_path = latest_artifact(runs_dir, "p4_temperature_*.json")
235	    if model_path is None or not Path(model_path).exists():
236	        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"P-4 model artifact not found in {runs_dir}"}
237	    if temperature_path is None or not Path(temperature_path).exists():
238	        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"P-4 temperature artifact not found in {runs_dir}"}
239	
240	    print(f"[P-5] using model: {model_path}", flush=True)
241	    print(f"[P-5] using temperature file: {temperature_path}", flush=True)
242	
243	    temp_meta = json.loads(Path(temperature_path).read_text())
244	    T = float(temp_meta["T"])
245	
246	    # Reconstruct hold-out exactly as in P-4.
247	    waveform_dir = reference_csv.parent
248	    df = pd.read_csv(reference_csv, header=None, names=["record_id", "label"])
249	    partition = json.loads(partition_json.read_text())
250	    dev_ids = set(partition["dev_record_ids"])
251	    df_dev = df[df["record_id"].isin(dev_ids)].copy()
252	    df_dev = df_dev[df_dev["label"] != "~"].reset_index(drop=True)
253	
254	    sss = StratifiedShuffleSplit(n_splits=1, test_size=0.20, random_state=42)
255	    split_iter = list(sss.split(df_dev["record_id"].values, df_dev["label"].values))
256	    _, holdout_idx = split_iter[0]
257	    holdout_records = df_dev.iloc[holdout_idx].copy().reset_index(drop=True)
258	
259	    label_map = {"N": 0, "A": 1, "O": 2, "~": 3}
260	    n_classes = 4
261	
262	    print(f"[P-5] hold-out records: {len(holdout_records)} (T={T:.3f})", flush=True)
263	    device = "cuda:0"
264	    model = build_xresnet1d50(in_channels=1, n_out=n_classes).to(device)
265	    state = torch.load(str(model_path), map_location=device, weights_only=False)
266	    if isinstance(state, dict) and "state_dict" in state:
267	        model.load_state_dict(state["state_dict"])
268	    else:
269	        model.load_state_dict(state)
270	    model.eval()
271	
272	    # Load + infer in batches without staging the full dataset in RAM
273	    # twice (P-4 already paid the load cost; we re-load here so P-5 is
274	    # standalone-runnable for re-checks).
275	    batch_size = 16
276	    all_logits = []
277	    all_y = []
278	    buf_x = []
279	    buf_y = []
280	    print("[P-5] loading hold-out + inference...", flush=True)
281	    t0 = time.perf_counter()
282	    n_failed = 0
283	    for i, (rid, lbl) in enumerate(zip(holdout_records["record_id"], holdout_records["label"])):
284	        try:
285	            sig = load_record_signal(waveform_dir / rid, target_len, fs)
286	        except Exception:
287	            n_failed += 1
288	            continue
289	        buf_x.append(sig)
290	        buf_y.append(label_map[lbl])
291	        if len(buf_x) == batch_size or i == len(holdout_records) - 1:
292	            x = torch.from_numpy(np.stack(buf_x).astype(np.float32)).unsqueeze(1).to(device)
293	            with torch.no_grad():
294	                logits = model(x)
295	            all_logits.append(logits.cpu().numpy())
296	            all_y.extend(buf_y)
297	            buf_x = []
298	            buf_y = []
299	        if (i + 1) % 200 == 0:
300	            print(f"  [infer] {i+1}/{len(holdout_records)}", flush=True)
301	    elapsed = time.perf_counter() - t0
302	    print(f"[P-5] inference complete in {elapsed:.1f}s (failed={n_failed})", flush=True)
303	
304	    logits = np.concatenate(all_logits, axis=0)
305	    y_holdout = np.asarray(all_y, dtype=np.int64)
306	
307	    probs_uncal = softmax(logits, axis=1)
308	    probs_cal = softmax(logits / max(T, 1e-3), axis=1)
309	
310	    # Brier scores (multi-class one-hot).
311	    y_onehot = np.zeros((len(y_holdout), n_classes), dtype=np.float32)
312	    y_onehot[np.arange(len(y_holdout)), y_holdout] = 1.0
313	    brier_uncal = float(((probs_uncal - y_onehot) ** 2).sum(axis=1).mean())
314	    brier_cal = float(((probs_cal - y_onehot) ** 2).sum(axis=1).mean())
315	
316	    # ECE.
317	    ece_uncal = compute_ece(probs_uncal, y_holdout, n_bins=n_bins)
318	    ece_cal = compute_ece(probs_cal, y_holdout, n_bins=n_bins)
319	
320	    # Reliability diagrams.
321	    bins_u, acc_u, conf_u, count_u = reliability_bins(probs_uncal, y_holdout, n_bins=n_bins)
322	    bins_c, acc_c, conf_c, count_c = reliability_bins(probs_cal, y_holdout, n_bins=n_bins)
323	
324	    ts = int(time.time())
325	    runs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
326	    png_path = runs_dir / f"p5_reliability_{ts}.png"
327	    plot_reliability(
328	        bins_u, acc_u, conf_u, count_u, acc_c, conf_c, count_c, T, png_path, ece_uncal, ece_cal
329	    )
330	    print(f"[P-5] saved reliability diagram -> {png_path}", flush=True)
331	
332	    # Success criteria.
333	    ece_improved = ece_cal < ece_uncal
334	    t_in_range = 1.0 <= T <= 5.0
335	    t_high_flag = T > 5.0
336	    # Reliability "closer to diagonal" measured via mean abs (acc - conf) over non-empty bins.
337	    def _mean_gap(acc, conf, count):
338	        gaps = []
339	        for a, c, n in zip(acc, conf, count):
340	            if a is not None and c is not None and n > 0:
341	                gaps.append(abs(a - c))
342	        return float(sum(gaps) / max(len(gaps), 1)) if gaps else None
343	
344	    gap_uncal = _mean_gap(acc_u, conf_u, count_u)
345	    gap_cal = _mean_gap(acc_c, conf_c, count_c)
346	    diagram_improved = (
347	        gap_uncal is not None and gap_cal is not None and gap_cal <= gap_uncal
348	    )
349	
350	    # PASS = ECE improves AND diagram improves AND T in [1,5] (T>5 is a
351	    # FLAG not a FAIL per the spec).
352	    status = "pass" if (ece_improved and diagram_improved and (t_in_range or t_high_flag)) else "fail"
353	
354	    return {
355	        "status": status,
356	        "n_holdout": int(len(y_holdout)),
357	        "n_failed_load": int(n_failed),
358	        "model_path": str(model_path),
359	        "temperature_path": str(temperature_path),
360	        "T": round(T, 4),
361	        "T_in_range_1_5": t_in_range,
362	        "T_high_flag_gt_5": t_high_flag,
363	        "brier_uncalibrated": round(brier_uncal, 5),
364	        "brier_calibrated": round(brier_cal, 5),
365	        "ece_uncalibrated": round(ece_uncal, 5),
366	        "ece_calibrated": round(ece_cal, 5),
367	        "ece_improved": ece_improved,
368	        "mean_reliability_gap_uncalibrated": (
369	            round(gap_uncal, 5) if gap_uncal is not None else None
370	        ),
371	        "mean_reliability_gap_calibrated": (
372	            round(gap_cal, 5) if gap_cal is not None else None
373	        ),
374	        "diagram_improved": diagram_improved,
375	        "reliability_png": str(png_path),
376	        "n_bins": n_bins,
377	        "reliability_uncalibrated": {
378	            "bin_edges": list(map(float, bins_u)),
379	            "accuracy": acc_u,
380	            "confidence": conf_u,
381	            "count": count_u,
382	        },
383	        "reliability_calibrated": {
384	            "bin_edges": list(map(float, bins_c)),
385	            "accuracy": acc_c,
386	            "confidence": conf_c,
387	            "count": count_c,
388	        },
389	    }
390

392	def main() -> int:
393	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="P-5 calibration baseline (reuses P-4 model)")
394	    ap.add_argument(
395	        "--cinc-dir",
396	        type=Path,
397	        default=Path.home() / "physionet_data" / "challenge-2017-1.0.0",
398	    )

400	        "--partition-json",

403	    )

405	        "--runs-dir",

408	    )
409	    ap.add_argument("--model-path", type=Path, default=None)

432	            "traceback": traceback.format_exc(),
433	        }
434	
435	    result["pilot"] = "P-5"
436	    result["spec"] = "20-plan/ecg-ppg-realworld/pilots-README.md#P-5"
437	
438	    out_path = args.out or (

440	    )

442	    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
443

def preprocess(signal, fs: int):
    import numpy as np
    from scipy.signal import butter, filtfilt, iirnotch

    sos_b, sos_a = butter(4, 0.5 / (fs / 2), btype="highpass")
    signal = filtfilt(sos_b, sos_a, signal)
    notch_b, notch_a = iirnotch(50.0 / (fs / 2), Q=30)
    signal = filtfilt(notch_b, notch_a, signal)
    signal = np.clip(signal, -5.0, 5.0)
    mu = float(signal.mean())
    sigma = float(signal.std())
    if sigma > 1e-9:
        signal = (signal - mu) / sigma
    return signal


def find_reference_csv(cinc_dir: Path):
    for cand in ("REFERENCE-v3.csv", "REFERENCE.csv", "training2017/REFERENCE-v3.csv", "training2017/REFERENCE.csv"):
        p = cinc_dir / cand
        if p.exists():
            return p
    return None


def build_xresnet1d50(in_channels: int = 1, n_out: int = 4):
    from fastai.vision.models.xresnet import ResBlock, XResNet

    return XResNet(
        ResBlock,
        expansion=4,
        layers=[3, 4, 6, 3],
        c_in=in_channels,
        n_out=n_out,
        ndim=1,
        ks=5,
    )


def pad_or_crop(signal, target_len: int):
    import numpy as np

    n = len(signal)
    if n == target_len:
        return signal.astype(np.float32)
    if n > target_len:
        start = (n - target_len) // 2
        return signal[start : start + target_len].astype(np.float32)
    out = np.zeros(target_len, dtype=np.float32)
    out[:n] = signal.astype(np.float32)
    return out


def load_record_signal(rec_path, target_len: int, fs: int):
    import wfdb

    r = wfdb.rdrecord(str(rec_path))
    sig = r.p_signal[:, 0]
    sig = preprocess(sig, fs)
    sig = pad_or_crop(sig, target_len)
    return sig


def latest_artifact(runs_dir: Path, pattern: str):
    cands = sorted(runs_dir.glob(pattern), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)
    return cands[-1] if cands else None


def compute_ece(probs, y_true, n_bins: int = 15) -> float:
    """Multi-class ECE: bin by max-confidence; per-bin gap = |acc - conf|."""
    import numpy as np

    pred = probs.argmax(axis=1)
    conf = probs.max(axis=1)
    correct = (pred == y_true).astype(float)

    bins = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1)
    ece = 0.0
    n = len(y_true)
    for i in range(n_bins):
        lo, hi = bins[i], bins[i + 1]
        # Last bin closed on right.
        if i == n_bins - 1:
            mask = (conf >= lo) & (conf <= hi)
        else:
            mask = (conf >= lo) & (conf < hi)
        if mask.sum() == 0:
            continue
        acc_bin = float(correct[mask].mean())
        conf_bin = float(conf[mask].mean())
        ece += (mask.sum() / n) * abs(acc_bin - conf_bin)
    return float(ece)


def reliability_bins(probs, y_true, n_bins: int = 15):
    import numpy as np

    pred = probs.argmax(axis=1)
    conf = probs.max(axis=1)
    correct = (pred == y_true).astype(float)
    bins = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1)
    bin_acc = []
    bin_conf = []
    bin_count = []
    for i in range(n_bins):
        lo, hi = bins[i], bins[i + 1]
        if i == n_bins - 1:
            mask = (conf >= lo) & (conf <= hi)
        else:
            mask = (conf >= lo) & (conf < hi)
        n_in = int(mask.sum())
        bin_count.append(n_in)
        if n_in == 0:
            bin_acc.append(None)
            bin_conf.append(None)
        else:
            bin_acc.append(float(correct[mask].mean()))
            bin_conf.append(float(conf[mask].mean()))
    return bins, bin_acc, bin_conf, bin_count


def plot_reliability(bins, acc_uncal, conf_uncal, count_uncal, acc_cal, conf_cal, count_cal, T, out_path: Path, ece_uncal: float, ece_cal: float):
    import matplotlib

    matplotlib.use("Agg")
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    import numpy as np

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 5))
    centers = (bins[:-1] + bins[1:]) / 2.0

    for ax, acc, conf, count, title, ece in (
        (axes[0], acc_uncal, conf_uncal, count_uncal, "uncalibrated", ece_uncal),
        (axes[1], acc_cal, conf_cal, count_cal, f"calibrated (T={T:.2f})", ece_cal),
    ):
        ax.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", linewidth=1, alpha=0.6)
        # Bars: x = bin center, height = bin acc.
        widths = 1.0 / len(centers)
        heights = [a if a is not None else 0.0 for a in acc]
        ax.bar(centers, heights, width=widths * 0.9, alpha=0.7, edgecolor="black", label="accuracy")
        # Overlay confidence as small markers.
        for c_x, c_y in zip(centers, conf):
            if c_y is not None:
                ax.plot(c_x, c_y, "rx", markersize=8)
        ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
        ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
        ax.set_xlabel("confidence")
        ax.set_ylabel("accuracy")
        ax.set_title(f"{title} — ECE={ece:.4f}")
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    fig.suptitle("Reliability diagrams — P-5 calibration baseline (CinC 2017 dev hold-out)")
    fig.tight_layout()
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=120)
    plt.close(fig)


def probe(
    cinc_dir: Path,
    partition_json: Path,
    runs_dir: Path,
    model_path: Path | None,
    temperature_path: Path | None,
    target_len: int,
    fs: int,
    n_bins: int,
):
    if not cinc_dir.exists():
        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"CinC 2017 dir not found at {cinc_dir}"}
    if not partition_json.exists():
        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"partition.json not found at {partition_json}"}

    reference_csv = find_reference_csv(cinc_dir)
    if reference_csv is None:
        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"REFERENCE csv not found under {cinc_dir}"}

    try:
        import numpy as np
        import pandas as pd
        import torch
        from scipy.special import softmax
        from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedShuffleSplit
    except ImportError as e:
        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"missing dep: {e}"}

    if not torch.cuda.is_available():
        return {"status": "fail", "reason": "torch.cuda unavailable; P-5 requires GPU."}

    # Auto-resolve P-4 artifacts.
    if model_path is None:
        model_path = latest_artifact(runs_dir, "p4_xresnet1d50_3ep_*.pt")
    if temperature_path is None:
        temperature_path = latest_artifact(runs_dir, "p4_temperature_*.json")
    if model_path is None or not Path(model_path).exists():
        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"P-4 model artifact not found in {runs_dir}"}
    if temperature_path is None or not Path(temperature_path).exists():
        return {"status": "fail", "reason": f"P-4 temperature artifact not found in {runs_dir}"}

    print(f"[P-5] using model: {model_path}", flush=True)
    print(f"[P-5] using temperature file: {temperature_path}", flush=True)

    temp_meta = json.loads(Path(temperature_path).read_text())
    T = float(temp_meta["T"])

    # Reconstruct hold-out exactly as in P-4.
    waveform_dir = reference_csv.parent
    df = pd.read_csv(reference_csv, header=None, names=["record_id", "label"])
    partition = json.loads(partition_json.read_text())
    dev_ids = set(partition["dev_record_ids"])
    df_dev = df[df["record_id"].isin(dev_ids)].copy()
    df_dev = df_dev[df_dev["label"] != "~"].reset_index(drop=True)

    sss = StratifiedShuffleSplit(n_splits=1, test_size=0.20, random_state=42)
    split_iter = list(sss.split(df_dev["record_id"].values, df_dev["label"].values))
    _, holdout_idx = split_iter[0]
    holdout_records = df_dev.iloc[holdout_idx].copy().reset_index(drop=True)

    label_map = {"N": 0, "A": 1, "O": 2, "~": 3}
    n_classes = 4

    print(f"[P-5] hold-out records: {len(holdout_records)} (T={T:.3f})", flush=True)
    device = "cuda:0"
    model = build_xresnet1d50(in_channels=1, n_out=n_classes).to(device)
    state = torch.load(str(model_path), map_location=device, weights_only=False)
    if isinstance(state, dict) and "state_dict" in state:
        model.load_state_dict(state["state_dict"])
    else:
        model.load_state_dict(state)
    model.eval()

    # Load + infer in batches without staging the full dataset in RAM
    # twice (P-4 already paid the load cost; we re-load here so P-5 is
    # standalone-runnable for re-checks).
    batch_size = 16
    all_logits = []
    all_y = []
    buf_x = []
    buf_y = []
    print("[P-5] loading hold-out + inference...", flush=True)
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    n_failed = 0
    for i, (rid, lbl) in enumerate(zip(holdout_records["record_id"], holdout_records["label"])):
        try:
            sig = load_record_signal(waveform_dir / rid, target_len, fs)
        except Exception:
            n_failed += 1
            continue
        buf_x.append(sig)
        buf_y.append(label_map[lbl])
        if len(buf_x) == batch_size or i == len(holdout_records) - 1:
            x = torch.from_numpy(np.stack(buf_x).astype(np.float32)).unsqueeze(1).to(device)
            with torch.no_grad():
                logits = model(x)
            all_logits.append(logits.cpu().numpy())
            all_y.extend(buf_y)
            buf_x = []
            buf_y = []
        if (i + 1) % 200 == 0:
            print(f"  [infer] {i+1}/{len(holdout_records)}", flush=True)
    elapsed = time.perf_counter() - t0
    print(f"[P-5] inference complete in {elapsed:.1f}s (failed={n_failed})", flush=True)

    logits = np.concatenate(all_logits, axis=0)
    y_holdout = np.asarray(all_y, dtype=np.int64)

    probs_uncal = softmax(logits, axis=1)
    probs_cal = softmax(logits / max(T, 1e-3), axis=1)

    # Brier scores (multi-class one-hot).
    y_onehot = np.zeros((len(y_holdout), n_classes), dtype=np.float32)
    y_onehot[np.arange(len(y_holdout)), y_holdout] = 1.0
    brier_uncal = float(((probs_uncal - y_onehot) ** 2).sum(axis=1).mean())
    brier_cal = float(((probs_cal - y_onehot) ** 2).sum(axis=1).mean())

    # ECE.
    ece_uncal = compute_ece(probs_uncal, y_holdout, n_bins=n_bins)
    ece_cal = compute_ece(probs_cal, y_holdout, n_bins=n_bins)

    # Reliability diagrams.
    bins_u, acc_u, conf_u, count_u = reliability_bins(probs_uncal, y_holdout, n_bins=n_bins)
    bins_c, acc_c, conf_c, count_c = reliability_bins(probs_cal, y_holdout, n_bins=n_bins)

    ts = int(time.time())
    runs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    png_path = runs_dir / f"p5_reliability_{ts}.png"
    plot_reliability(
        bins_u, acc_u, conf_u, count_u, acc_c, conf_c, count_c, T, png_path, ece_uncal, ece_cal
    )
    print(f"[P-5] saved reliability diagram -> {png_path}", flush=True)

    # Success criteria.
    ece_improved = ece_cal < ece_uncal
    t_in_range = 1.0 <= T <= 5.0
    t_high_flag = T > 5.0
    # Reliability "closer to diagonal" measured via mean abs (acc - conf) over non-empty bins.
    def _mean_gap(acc, conf, count):
        gaps = []
        for a, c, n in zip(acc, conf, count):
            if a is not None and c is not None and n > 0:
                gaps.append(abs(a - c))
        return float(sum(gaps) / max(len(gaps), 1)) if gaps else None

    gap_uncal = _mean_gap(acc_u, conf_u, count_u)
    gap_cal = _mean_gap(acc_c, conf_c, count_c)
    diagram_improved = (
        gap_uncal is not None and gap_cal is not None and gap_cal <= gap_uncal
    )

    # PASS = ECE improves AND diagram improves AND T in [1,5] (T>5 is a
    # FLAG not a FAIL per the spec).
    status = "pass" if (ece_improved and diagram_improved and (t_in_range or t_high_flag)) else "fail"

    return {
        "status": status,
        "n_holdout": int(len(y_holdout)),
        "n_failed_load": int(n_failed),
        "model_path": str(model_path),
        "temperature_path": str(temperature_path),
        "T": round(T, 4),
        "T_in_range_1_5": t_in_range,
        "T_high_flag_gt_5": t_high_flag,
        "brier_uncalibrated": round(brier_uncal, 5),
        "brier_calibrated": round(brier_cal, 5),
        "ece_uncalibrated": round(ece_uncal, 5),
        "ece_calibrated": round(ece_cal, 5),
        "ece_improved": ece_improved,
        "mean_reliability_gap_uncalibrated": (
            round(gap_uncal, 5) if gap_uncal is not None else None
        ),
        "mean_reliability_gap_calibrated": (
            round(gap_cal, 5) if gap_cal is not None else None
        ),
        "diagram_improved": diagram_improved,
        "reliability_png": str(png_path),
        "n_bins": n_bins,
        "reliability_uncalibrated": {
            "bin_edges": list(map(float, bins_u)),
            "accuracy": acc_u,
            "confidence": conf_u,
            "count": count_u,
        },
        "reliability_calibrated": {
            "bin_edges": list(map(float, bins_c)),
            "accuracy": acc_c,
            "confidence": conf_c,
            "count": count_c,
        },
    }


def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="P-5 calibration baseline (reuses P-4 model)")
    ap.add_argument(
        "--cinc-dir",
        type=Path,
        default=Path.home() / "physionet_data" / "challenge-2017-1.0.0",
    )
    ap.add_argument(
        "--partition-json",
        type=Path,
        default=Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "runs" / "partition.json",
    )
    ap.add_argument(
        "--runs-dir",
        type=Path,
        default=Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "runs",
    )
    ap.add_argument("--model-path", type=Path, default=None)
    ap.add_argument("--temperature-path", type=Path, default=None)
    ap.add_argument("--target-len", type=int, default=9000)
    ap.add_argument("--fs", type=int, default=300)
    ap.add_argument("--n-bins", type=int, default=15)
    ap.add_argument("--out", type=Path, default=None)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    try:
        result = probe(
            args.cinc_dir,
            args.partition_json,
            args.runs_dir,
            args.model_path,
            args.temperature_path,
            args.target_len,
            args.fs,
            args.n_bins,
        )
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
        result = {
            "status": "fail",
            "reason": f"unhandled exception: {e}",
            "traceback": traceback.format_exc(),
        }

    result["pilot"] = "P-5"
    result["spec"] = "20-plan/ecg-ppg-realworld/pilots-README.md#P-5"

    out_path = args.out or (
        args.runs_dir / f"pilot_p5_calibration_{int(time.time())}.json"
    )
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

    summary_keys = [
        "status",
        "n_holdout",
        "n_failed_load",
        "T",
        "T_in_range_1_5",
        "T_high_flag_gt_5",
        "brier_uncalibrated",
        "brier_calibrated",
        "ece_uncalibrated",
        "ece_calibrated",
        "ece_improved",
        "mean_reliability_gap_uncalibrated",
        "mean_reliability_gap_calibrated",
        "diagram_improved",
        "reliability_png",
        "model_path",
        "temperature_path",
        "reason",
    ]
    summary = {k: result.get(k) for k in summary_keys if k in result}
    print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))
    print(f"\nWrote {out_path}")
    return 0 if result.get("status") == "pass" else 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

content.content:
1	> **Spec:** `10-pain-point/sleep-staging/admission.md`
2	
3	# Risk register — sleep-staging
4	
5	**Track:** sleep-staging
6	**Date:** 2026-05-02 (initial); revised 2026-05-03
7	**Author:** methodologist agent
8	**Status:** revised — see unlock-note-2026-05-03.md
9	
10	Each risk: description, probability (H/M/L), impact (H/M/L), mitigation, kill criterion.
11	Kill criteria are threshold-triggered: hitting one stops the affected arm immediately.
12	
13	The track has two arms — (b) HRV-only primary and (a) pretrained-stager (now co-primary).
14	
15	---
16	
17	## R-1 — NSRR DUA not submitted or not received in time (scope a)
18	
19	**Status: RESOLVED — 2026-05-03**
20	
21	**Resolution:** NSRR API token is available in the project environment as `NSRR_TOKEN`
22	in `.env` (gitignored). Token issuance by NSRR confirms that the DUA has been approved.
23	SHHS and MESA are immediately accessible. The 4-week kill criterion no longer applies.
24	
25	**What to do at implementation start:** Validate token with a test request (P-7 pilot).
26	Log token validity confirmation in `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/nsrr_token_validation.txt`.
27	
28	**Kill criterion: REMOVED.** The 4-week clock and scope (a) conditional-cancel are
29	no longer in effect. Scope (a) begins in week 1.
30	
31	**Residual risk (R-1a, new): NSRR token expires or is invalidated.**
32	Probability: L. Impact: H for scope (a). Mitigation: validate token at start of every
33	session that downloads NSRR data. If token is rejected, re-login to sleepdata.org to
34	renew. Kill criterion: if token cannot be renewed within 3 business days and no MESA data
35	has been downloaded, defer scope (a) and fall back to scope (b) standalone (same outcome
36	as the original kill criterion).
37	
38	---
39	
40	## R-2 — HMC PSG access requires multi-week DUA or is unavailable
41	
42	**Status: TRIGGERED and RESOLVED via substitution specification — 2026-05-03**
43	
44	**Trigger:** User reports uncertainty about credentialed-access timeline.
45	
46	**Resolution from verification:** HMC Sleep Staging Database is CC BY 4.0 open access.
47	The PhysioNet page explicitly states "Anyone can access the files … Creative Commons
48	Attribution 4.0 International Public License." There is no DUA and no multi-week wait.
49	The original R-2 probability was based on conservative uncertainty about the dataset's
50	tier; that uncertainty is now resolved.
51	
52	**Despite resolution, full CAP Sleep substitute specification is included here** because:
53	(a) the user reported a real access concern in practice, (b) access barriers can arise
54	from institutional network configurations or PhysioNet account issues independent of
55	license tier, and (c) having a fully specified substitute eliminates operational delay
56	if a barrier materializes.
57	
58	**Updated probability:** L (HMC is open access confirmed; barrier now requires some
59	local/institutional factor).
60	**Impact:** H for scope (b).
61	
62	**Mitigation:**
63	
64	1. Run P-1 (HMC access check) as the first action in week 1. Record access confirmation
65	   in `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/hmc_access_tier.txt`.
66	
67	2. **If HMC PSG is inaccessible** for any reason after P-1:
68	   Activate CAP Sleep substitute. CAP Sleep Database at
69	   https://physionet.org/content/capslpdb/1.0.0/ is ODC-BY open access (confirmed
70	   2026-05-03). 108 subjects, ECG present in all recordings.
71	
72	   **CAP-specific preprocessing requirement:** R&K stage labels. Apply standard mapping:
73	   W→W, S1→N1, S2→N2, {S3, S4}→N3, REM→REM. Implement in a dedicated
74	   `rk_to_aasm_map()` utility in the track-specific loader. Verify the mapping is
75	   applied consistently in both training and evaluation.
76	
77	   **CAP pathology caveat:** Only 4 of 108 CAP subjects have sleep-disordered breathing.
78	   AHI-band stratification is not feasible. If CAP is activated, the AHI-stratified
79	   secondary analysis is dropped. Pathology-category stratification (healthy / NFLE /
80	   RBD / PLM / insomnia / other) replaces it.
81	
82	   **CAP partition:** 54 dev / 54 test, stratified by pathology category, seed=42.
83	   Re-freeze protocol-lock.md §3 with CAP-specific partition before any training.
84	
85	3. Log substitution activation in `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/cap_substitute_activation.txt`
86	   before any model training if CAP is activated.
87	
88	**Kill criterion (unchanged in structure):** If HMC PSG access requires a DUA that will
89	not be approved within 2 weeks, AND CAP Sleep is also inaccessible (e.g., PhysioNet
90	entirely down), AND no other substitute dataset with ≥ 54 subjects with ECG channels
91	is available open-access, retire-cancel scope (b) primary arm. The track may pivot to
92	scope (a) only if MESA access is confirmed. Document the substitution attempt.
93	
94	---
95	
96	## R-3 — ECG channel absent or unusable in HMC PSG subjects
97	
98	Unchanged from original risk register. SQI threshold > 30 % low-quality windows → exclude.
99	Exclusion count reported explicitly.
100	
101	**Supplement for CAP Sleep (if substitute active):** CAP Sleep ECG channels are present
102	per the dataset description but quality is not documented at per-subject granularity.
103	Apply the same SQI pipeline. If exclusion reduces usable subjects below 40 in the test
104	split, downgrade the claim scope as per the original kill criterion.
105	
106	**Kill criterion (original):** Unchanged. If ECG quality exclusions leave fewer than 40
107	usable test subjects, either (a) supplement with CAP Sleep data, or (b) report with
108	explicit underpowered caveat. Does not auto-trigger retire-cancel.
109	
110	---
111	
112	## R-4 — U-Sleep checkpoint unavailable or license-incompatible
113	
114	Unchanged from original risk register.
115	
116	**Additional note (2026-05-03):** U-Sleep's training corpus (Perslev et al. 2021
117	supplementary, 16 studies, 15,660 PSGs) must be checked against MESA. If MESA is in
118	U-Sleep's training data, scope (a) EEG evaluation on MESA is contaminated (the model
119	saw these subjects during training). This check is required in the pre-run checklist
120	for scope (a) (protocol-lock.md §6 HEADLINE-A checklist).
121	
122	**Kill criterion (unchanged):** If no pretrained EEG stager checkpoint can be obtained,
123	cancel the pretrained-stager comparison from scope (a) and fall back to 1D-ResNet for
124	scope (b) EEG comparison. If the 1D-ResNet fallback also fails, retire-cancel scope (a)
125	and proceed with scope (b) alone.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## R-5 — U-Sleep inference exceeds 4 GB VRAM on GTX 1650
130	
131	Unchanged from original risk register. P-3 pilot verifies at batch=32. If > 3 GB:
132	reduce batch size. If > 3 GB at batch=1: move to Kaggle T4.
133	
134	**Additional note for MESA-scale inference:** MESA involves ~1,850 test subjects
135	(~1.85 M epochs). Even at batch=32, this is a multi-day job on GTX 1650. If Kaggle T4
136	is used for MESA inference, probability arrays (soft predictions per epoch) are
137	extracted in Kaggle and downloaded locally (~50 MB per subject at 5-class float32
138	× 1000 epochs = 20 KB; total ~100 MB — download feasible). The headline run is then
139	the evaluation/statistics step, which runs locally on CPU.
140	
141	**Kill criterion (unchanged):** If inference fails at all batch sizes locally AND Kaggle
142	T4 fails within 2 days, cancel the U-Sleep EEG comparison from scope (a) and scope (b).
143	Scope (b) reports HRV-only RF results without direct EEG comparison, noting the gap
144	claim cannot be quantified at this resource level.
145	
146	---
147	
148	## R-6 — Dreem-DOD download too large for local storage
149	
150	Unchanged from original risk register. Dreem-DOD (58 GB) is for P-6 framing probe only
151	— optional given MESA is now available. If local storage is insufficient, Dreem-DOD is
152	deprioritized; MESA via NSRR is the primary scope (a) dataset regardless.
153	
154	**Kill criterion (unchanged):** Drop Dreem-DOD OOD probe if inaccessible both locally
155	and via Kaggle within week 2. Scope (b) and scope (a) MESA are unaffected.
156	
157	---
158	
159	## R-7 — HRV features are insufficient to discriminate N1 from N2
160	
161	Unchanged from original risk register. Expected pre-specified outcome; N1 F1 near zero
162	is valid and informative. No kill criterion.
163	
164	---
165	
166	## R-8 — HRV-only staging matches EEG (scope b null result)
167	
168	Unchanged from original risk register. Pre-specified defensible-null. No kill criterion.
169	
170	---
171	
172	## R-9 — Held-out partition touched prematurely (protocol violation)
173	
174	Unchanged from original risk register.
175	
176	**Additional note (2026-05-03):** There are now TWO held-out partitions (HMC PSG test
177	split for scope b; MESA test partition for scope a). Both are governed by separate
178	pre-run checklists. The isolation requirement applies to each independently. Subject-ID
179	lists for each partition are stored in separate files:
180	- `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/partition_definition_hmcpsg.json`
181	- `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/partition_definition_mesa.json`
182	
183	**Kill criterion (unchanged):** If a model selection decision was made after seeing
184	test-split performance for either partition, retire-cancel the corresponding headline.
185	Applies independently to each scope.
186	
187	---
188	
189	## R-10 — Stage label quality in HMC PSG (single-rater labels)
190	
191	Unchanged from original risk register. Documented limitation; does not affect the paired
192	EEG-vs-HRV gap claim. No kill criterion.
193	
194	---
195	
196	## R-11 — Dreem-DOD multi-rater consensus labels — version compatibility
197	
198	Unchanged from original risk register. Lower priority now that MESA is available.
199	Kill criterion: drop Dreem-DOD OOD probe if format incompatibility unfixable in 2 days.
200	
201	---
202	
203	## R-12 — CAP Sleep R&K-to-AASM label mapping error (NEW — 2026-05-03)
204	
205	**Description:** CAP Sleep uses R&K staging (W, S1, S2, S3, S4, REM, plus "MT" for
206	body movements). If the standard AASM-equivalent mapping (S1→N1, S2→N2, {S3,S4}→N3)
207	is applied inconsistently between the training set label encoding and the evaluation
208	label encoding, the classifier will be evaluated on a different label space than it
209	was trained on, producing artificially degraded results.
210	
211	**Applies to:** Scope (b) only, and only if CAP Sleep substitute is activated.
212	
213	**Probability:** M (only if substitute is activated; the mapping itself is well-defined
214	and standardized, but implementation errors are easy to make in the split of 6 R&K
215	classes to 5 AASM classes, especially for the body-movement "MT" label).
216	**Impact:** M — if inconsistent, results are invalid and training must be restarted.
217	
218	**Mitigation:**
219	1. Implement `rk_to_aasm_map(stage_label)` as a standalone function with an explicit
220	   lookup table and a test: `{W→W, S1→N1, S2→N2, S3→N3, S4→N3, REM→REM, MT→None}`.
221	   MT (body movements) → None means that epoch is excluded from both training and
222	   evaluation. Document the exclusion fraction.
223	2. Apply the mapping once, at load time, before any train/test split. Verify the
224	   label distribution after mapping matches expected proportions (no S3, S4, MT in
225	   the output).
226	3. Add an assertion in the training script: `assert set(labels).issubset({'W','N1','N2','N3','REM'})`.
227	
228	**Kill criterion:** If the R&K-to-AASM mapping produces a label distribution inconsistent
229	with expected sleep staging proportions (e.g., NREM > 70 % of epochs, or REM < 5 %),
230	flag as a mapping error and halt before any training. Investigate the loader before
231	proceeding.
232	
233	---
234	
235	## R-13 — MESA storage and download bottleneck (NEW — 2026-05-03)
236	
237	**Description:** MESA PSG EDF files total approximately 165 GB (2,056 subjects × ~80 MB).
238	Local disk may not accommodate the full dataset simultaneously. NSRR bandwidth throttling
239	may also make bulk download impractical in the week-1 timeline.
240	
241	**Probability:** M (165 GB is a significant local storage requirement; bandwidth to NSRR
242	servers is unknown).
243	**Impact:** M — delays scope (a) headline by 1–2 weeks if download is slow.
244	
245	**Mitigation:**
246	1. Check local disk space before initiating MESA bulk download. 200 GB free required.
247	2. If insufficient local storage: download in subject batches. Process each batch
248	   (ECG/EEG extraction → probability arrays via U-Sleep), save probability arrays
249	   (~20 KB per subject), delete EDFs. The probability arrays (~40 MB total for 2,056
250	   subjects) are the only artifact needed for evaluation.
251	3. If NSRR throttles download to < 5 MB/s: use the nsrr-gem parallel download flag
252	   or break into batches of 200 subjects/session (each batch ~16 GB, ~1 hr at 5 MB/s).
253	4. Kaggle fallback: mount NSRR files in a Kaggle notebook (nsrr-gem supports Kaggle
254	   environments with token injection), extract probabilities in notebook, download
255	   results. Only if local storage is genuinely insufficient.
256	
257	**Kill criterion:** If MESA download cannot complete within 2 weeks of week 1 start,
258	scope (a) headline is delayed but not cancelled. If after 4 weeks only a subset of
259	MESA subjects has been downloaded and the subset is < 500 subjects, re-evaluate whether
260	a reduced-N scope (a) analysis (N=500) is adequate for the OSA-stratification claim
261	(smallest stratum at N=500 × 15 % = ~75 severe OSA subjects — borderline for 10 pp
262	gap at 80 % power). If N is borderline, report with explicit underpowered caveat.
263	
264	---
265	
266	## Summary table (revised)
267	
268	| ID | Risk | Arm | P | I | Status | Kill criterion |
269	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
270	| R-1 | NSRR DUA not received | (a) | — | — | **RESOLVED** | Kill removed; residual R-1a (token renewal) |
271	| R-1a | NSRR token expired/invalid | (a) | L | H | Open | Defer scope (a) if unresolvable in 3 days |
272	| R-2 | HMC PSG access blocked | (b) primary | L | H | **RESOLVED** (confirmed open); substitute fully specified | Retire-cancel scope (b) if no DUA-free substitute ≥54 subj |
273	| R-3 | ECG quality exclusions | (b) | M | M | Open | Downgrade claim; supplement with CAP if < 40 test subj |
274	| R-4 | U-Sleep checkpoint unavailable | (a)/(b) | L–M | H | Open | Fall back to 1D-ResNet; if that fails, cancel EEG comparison |
275	| R-5 | U-Sleep VRAM > 3 GB | (a)/(b) | M | M | Open | Move to Kaggle T4; if T4 fails, cancel EEG comparison |
276	| R-6 | Dreem-DOD download impractical | (a) | M | L | Open (lower priority) | Drop Dreem-DOD probe; MESA unaffected |
277	| R-7 | HRV N1 F1 near zero | (b) | H | L | Open | None — valid pre-specified outcome |
278	| R-8 | HRV-only matches EEG | (b) | L | L | Open | None — valid pre-specified outcome |
279	| R-9 | Held-out partition touched prematurely | both | L–M | H | Open | Retire-cancel the affected scope's headline |
280	| R-10 | Single-rater HMC PSG labels | (b) | H | M | Open | None — documented limitation |
281	| R-11 | Dreem-DOD format mismatch | (a) | M | L | Open (lower priority) | Drop Dreem-DOD probe if unfixable in 2 days |
282	| R-12 | CAP R&K-to-AASM mapping error | (b) if substitute active | M | M | **NEW** | Halt training if label distribution implausible |
283	| R-13 | MESA storage / download bottleneck | (a) | M | M | **NEW** | Delay headline; reduce to N≥500 with caveat if 4 wks elapse |
284	
285	---
286	
287	## Retire-cancel triggers (track level)
288	
289	The entire track retires-cancelled if:
290	
291	1. **Primary data inaccessible:** HMC PSG and CAP Sleep both inaccessible within 2 weeks,
292	   AND MESA/SHHS scope (a) data has not been downloaded, leaving no executable scope.
293	
294	2. **Protocol violation (R-9 severe case):** The held-out partition for any scope was used
295	   for model selection. Hard cancel for that scope, no recovery.
296	
297	3. **ECG entirely absent from all accessible datasets:** HRV feature extraction infeasible
298	   across all datasets. Retire-cancel with documented reason.
299	
300	In any retire-cancel scenario: write retire-cancel note in `10-pain-point/sleep-staging/admission.md`,
301	update `10-pain-point/shared/portfolio.md`, tag `v2-sleep-staging-retired-cancelled`. Promote
302	any reusable substrate (hrv_features.py, calibration.py, cohort_stratifier.py) built before
303	cancellation if ≥1 plausible second consumer exists.
304
